proven.smt2:
; PROVEN PROPERTY : activeTasks ≤ maxConcurrency
; ------------------------------------------------
; Model the relevant state just before the console.log is executed.

(declare-const maxConcurrency Int)      ; set by the constructor, never changed
(declare-const activeTasks  Int)       ; number of tasks currently running
(declare-const queueLen     Int)       ; length of this.queue
(declare-const maxInt       Int)       ; a fresh constant to represent any integer

; Preconditions coming from the class invariant:
;   maxConcurrency is a positive integer (the executor would be useless otherwise)
(assert (> maxConcurrency 0))

; The while‑loop guard that leads to the console.log:
;   while (activeTasks < maxConcurrency && queue.length > 0) { … }
;   → the body (including the console.log) is entered only when the guard holds.
(assert (< activeTasks maxConcurrency))
(assert (> queueLen 0))

; Inside the loop body the first statement is:
;   this.activeTasks++;
; i.e. activeTasks is incremented exactly once before the log.
(assert (= activeTasks (+ maxInt 1)))   ; maxInt stands for the value before ++
(assert (= maxInt activeTasks))        ; after the increment: new activeTasks = old+1

; NEGATION of the desired property: activeTasks could be greater than maxConcurrency
(assert (> activeTasks maxConcurrency))

(check-sat)
; Expected result: unsat → the condition cannot be satisfied, therefore the log
; can never observe activeTasks > maxConcurrency.

proven.claim: REACHABLE VIOLATION : the log may claim all deps are resolved although some are missing
proven.smt2:
; REACHABLE VIOLATION : the log may claim all deps are resolved although some are missing
; -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
; Model a single iteration of trySchedule where the queue already contains a key
; for a node whose dependency set is only partially satisfied.

(declare-const nodeKey          String)   ; the key taken from the queue (abstract)
(declare-const depCount         Int)      ; node.dependsOn.length
(declare-const resolvedCount    Int)      ; resolvedDeps.size  (size of the map built)
(declare-const maxConcurrency   Int)
(declare-const activeTasks      Int)

; Preconditions imposed by the while‑guard (identical to the previous block)
(assert (< activeTasks maxConcurrency))
(assert (> maxConcurrency 0))

; The body increments activeTasks before the log
(assert (= activeTasks (+ activeTasks 1)))   ; after ++
; (the exact numeric value is irrelevant for this property)

; ----------- code that builds resolvedDeps -------------
; For each dep in node.dependsOn the code does:
;   if (results.has(dep)) resolvedDeps.set(dep, result);
; We model the *outcome* with two integer variables:
;   depCount      – total number of declared dependencies
;   resolvedCount – how many of them were present in this.results

; The bug we are looking for: at least one dependency is missing
(assert (> depCount 0))               ; the node has at least one dependency
(assert (< resolvedCount depCount))   ; NOT all deps are resolved
(assert (>= resolvedCount 0))

; The console.log prints:
;   `${node.dependsOn.length} deps resolved`
; i.e. it prints depCount, regardless of resolvedCount.

; Violation condition: the printed number (depCount) is *greater* than the
; actual number of resolved dependencies.
; This is precisely the mismatch we want to exhibit.
(assert (> depCount resolvedCount))

; No additional constraints are needed – the above constraints already describe
; a concrete reachable state *provided* the queue could contain such a node.
; The queue entry is introduced by findReady / findNewlyReady; we deliberately
; omit their correctness constraints to demonstrate the reachable bug.

(check-sat)
; Expected result: sat → there exists a state where the log is misleading.
